task_completion.description: Locate the CLEANING_ROBOT in the simulation world.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Webots scene tree showing 'DEF CLEANING_ROBOT Robot' expanded, or a text editor open to the .wbt file with the cursor/view near the 'CLEANING_ROBOT' definition.

task_completion.description: Set the left_encoder resolution and noise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'left_encoder' device in the Webots GUI or text editor with its 'resolution' field set to 0.0123 and 'noise' field set to 0.015.

task_completion.description: Set the right_encoder resolution and noise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'right_encoder' device in the Webots GUI or text editor with its 'resolution' field set to 0.0123 and 'noise' field set to 0.015.

task_completion.description: Set the ir_front sensor resolution and noise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'ir_front' DistanceSensor in the Webots GUI or text editor with its 'resolution' field set to 0.002 and 'noise' field set to 0.05.

task_completion.description: Set the ir_left and ir_right sensor resolution and noise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for both the 'ir_left' and 'ir_right' DistanceSensors in the Webots GUI or text editor with their 'resolution' fields set to 0.002 and 'noise' fields set to 0.05.

task_completion.description: Save the modified world to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save As' dialog in Webots saving to '/home/ga/Desktop/noisy_slam_test.wbt', a terminal command executing the save/copy to this path, or the text editor/Webots window title reflecting the new 'noisy_slam_test.wbt' filename.

integrity.description: The agent used appropriate tools (Webots GUI or a text editor) to view and modify the world file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Webots application or a text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) open with the world file contents visible, rather than blindly running sed/echo commands without checking the file structure.

integrity.description: The agent explicitly entered the requested noise and resolution values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots capturing the agent typing or modifying the specific numerical values (0.0123, 0.015, 0.002, 0.05) in the relevant fields.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the save operation to the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for confirmation of the file being written to '/home/ga/Desktop/', such as a file explorer view, an 'ls' command showing the new file, or the successful completion of a save dialog.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output path must be exactly '/home/ga/Desktop/noisy_slam_test.wbt'. The robot's DEF name is 'CLEANING_ROBOT'. The required values are: encoder resolution = 0.0123, encoder noise = 0.015, IR sensor resolution = 0.002, and IR sensor noise = 0.05.
